972	\begin{lemma}
973	[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is geometrically irreducible]
974	  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}

976	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
977	  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
978	  % II.4 + I.1; \(\Proj\) of an integral domain over a field is
979	  % integral and geometrically integral when the field is algebraically
980	  % closed and the polynomial ring is well-defined). No external SOURCE
981	  % QUOTE needed.
982	  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
983	  .\mathrm{hom} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is
984	  geometrically irreducible: for every field extension \(K/\bar k\),
985	  the base change \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \times_{\Spec \bar k} \Spec K
986	  = \mathbb P^1_K\) is irreducible. Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically
987	  closed and \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is the polynomial ring (integral
988	  domain), \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is
989	  integral, and the base change to any field extension \(K\) preserves
990	  integrality (the homogeneous coordinate ring \(K[X_0, X_1]\) is
991	  itself an integral domain, hence \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is integral and
992	  irreducible).
993	\end{lemma}

1001	  \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\), recording the multiplication \((x, y) \mapsto xy\) and unit \(1\)
1002	  of \cref{def:gm} as a Lean \(\mathtt{GrpObj}\) instance. This structure is consumed only as a
1003	  carrier for the scaling action \(\sigma_\times\) of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}; the primary
1004	  \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling proof of \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} uses \(\mathbb G_m\) as a
1005	  parameter scheme (via \(W\) in the additive decomposition), not its group-object axioms.
1006	\end{definition}
1007	\subsection*{Chart cover and chart-ring iso (formalisation infrastructure)}
1008	The next three declarations are iter-168 formalisation infrastructure that the chartwise
1009	construction of the \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling action \(\sigma_\times\) (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1})
1010	and its downstream consumers (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}) ultimately rest on.
1011	They realise, at the Lean level, the standard ``affine chart of \(\mathbb P^1\) is $\mathbb
1012	A^1\('' picture: a two-chart affine open cover of \)\overline{\mathbb P^1}\( by \)D_+(X_0)$ and
1013	\(D_+(X_1)\); the identification of each chart ring with the one-variable polynomial ring
1014	\(\bar k[u]\); and the resulting reducedness of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) as a scheme. None
1015	of them is on the genus-\(0\) critical path conceptually, but each is consumed by the Lean
1016	formalisation downstream.
1017	\begin{definition}
1018	[Two-chart affine open cover of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\)]
1019	  \label{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1020	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover}
1021	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of the textbook fact that \(\mathbb P^1\) is covered by its
1022	  % two standard affine charts \(D_+(X_0)\) and \(D_+(X_1)\); the construction is built on top of
1023	  % Mathlib's \(\mathrm{Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan}\). No external % SOURCE QUOTE
1024	  % needed for the Lean encoding itself.
1025	  The two-chart affine open cover of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) by the standard \(D_+(X_0)\) and
1026	  \(D_+(X_1)\), built by specialising Mathlib's
1027	  \(\mathrm{Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan}\) to the index set
1028	  \(\iota = \{0, 1\} = \mathrm{Fin}\,2\) with family \(f = (X_0, X_1)\) and weights
1029	  \(m = (1, 1)\). The non-trivial input is that the irrelevant ideal of the bi-graded
1030	  \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is contained in \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\{X_0, X_1\}\), which holds by
1031	  writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials of strictly positive total degree
1032	  (so each monomial is divisible by \(X_0\) or \(X_1\)). This cover packages the two affine
1033	  charts into the data that Mathlib's \(\mathrm{IsReduced.of\_openCover}\) and
1034	  \(\mathrm{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}\) APIs consume, and is the chartwise glue for the
1035	  iter-169 attack on the body of \(\sigma_\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}\)
1036	  (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}).
1037	\end{definition}
1038	\begin{definition}
1039	\leanok
1040	[Chart-ring iso: \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \cong \bar k[u]\)]
1041	  \label{def:proj_chart_ring_iso}
1042	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}
1043	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of the textbook identification ``the affine chart of
1044	  % \(\mathbb P^1\) at \(X_i\) is \(\mathbb A^1\) with coordinate \(X_{1-i}/X_i\)'', expressed at
1045	  % the homogeneous-localization level. No external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1046	  For each \(i \in \{0, 1\}\), the chart-\(i\) ring of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) --- the
1047	  degree-zero part \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) of the localization of the standard
1048	  \(\mathbb N\)-graded polynomial ring \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) at \(X_i\) --- is isomorphic as a
1049	  \(\bar k\)-algebra to the one-variable polynomial ring \(\bar k[u]\), where the affine
1050	  chart coordinate is \(u = X_{1-i}/X_i\). This is the formalisation-level realisation of
1051	  the standard ``affine chart of \(\mathbb P^1\) is \(\mathbb A^1\)'' identification expressed
1052	  at the homogeneous-localization level. The forward direction $\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal
1053	  A\,X_i \to \bar k[u]\( is built via \)\mathrm{Localization.awayLift}$ from the chart
1054	  evaluation \(X_i \mapsto 1\), \(X_{1-i} \mapsto u\); the inverse direction is built via the
1055	  universal property of \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k\), sending \(u\) to the
1056	  homogeneous-localization element \(X_{1-i}/X_i \in \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\); the
1057	  two directions are packaged as a \(\mathrm{RingEquiv}\) via \(\mathrm{RingEquiv.ofRingHom}\)
1058	  applied to the two round-trip identities.
1059	  % NOTE (iter-172 refresh of iter-171): the reverse round-trip body
1060	  % \cref{lem:proj_chart_ring_iso_aux_left} is a REAL ``cancel surjective'' proof
1061	  % (axiom-clean once the surjectivity helper is closed), and the residual
1062	  % \texttt{sorryAx} taint of the iso now flows entirely through the named
1063	  % helper \cref{lem:mvPoly_to_homogeneousLocalization_away_surjective}
1064	  % (Lean: \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway\_surjective},
1065	  % \texttt{Genus0BaseObjects.lean:372}). The forward round-trip
1066	  % (the identity on \(\bar k[u]\)) remains axiom-clean. Closing the surjectivity
1067	  % helper (via \texttt{Away.adjoin\_mk\_prod\_pow\_eq\_top} specialised to \(d = 1\),
1068	  % \(v = (X_0, X_1)\), \(dv = (1, 1)\)) discharges the chart-ring iso's last sorry.
1069	\end{definition}
1070	\begin{lemma}
1071	\leanok
1072	[Reverse round-trip of the chart-ring iso]
1073	  \label{lem:proj_chart_ring_iso_aux_left}
1074	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left}
1075	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; this is the residual identity needed for the chart-ring
1076	  % map of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} to upgrade from a pair of mutually inverse-looking
1077	  % ring maps to a genuine \(\mathrm{RingEquiv}\). No external % SOURCE QUOTE.
1078	  The composite $\mathrm{inverse} \circ \mathrm{forward} \colon \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal
1079	  A\,X_i \to \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$ is the identity ring map. This is the reverse
1080	  half of the round-trip witnessing that the chart-ring map of
1081	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} is a ring isomorphism; the forward half $\mathrm{forward}
1082	  \circ \mathrm{inverse} = \mathrm{id}_{\bar k[u]}$ is already proven axiom-clean.
1083	  As of iter-171, this reverse round-trip body has \emph{landed} as a real
1084	  ``cancel surjective'' proof: from surjectivity of the inverse
1085	  \(\bar k[u] \to \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\)
1086	  (\cref{lem:mvPoly_to_homogeneousLocalization_away_surjective}) and the already-proven
1087	  forward round-trip \(\mathrm{forward} \circ \mathrm{inverse} = \mathrm{id}_{\bar k[u]}\)
1088	  one cancels to conclude \(\mathrm{inverse} \circ \mathrm{forward} = \mathrm{id}\) on the
1089	  \(\mathrm{Away}\) side. The cancellation step itself is mechanical; the only remaining
1090	  substantive content is the surjectivity sub-lemma below.
1091	\end{lemma}
1092	\begin{lemma}
1093	\leanok
1094	[Surjectivity of the inverse chart-ring map \(\bar k[u] \to \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\)]
1095	  \label{lem:mvPoly_to_homogeneousLocalization_away_surjective}
1096	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective}
1097	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of an elementary corollary of a Mathlib lemma. The
1098	  % external authority is the Mathlib statement
1099	  % \texttt{HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin\_mk\_prod\_pow\_eq\_top}
1100	  % (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:1064}); we do
1101	  % not include a verbatim \% SOURCE QUOTE because no Mathlib source quote is currently
1102	  % retained under \texttt{references/} for this lemma (Mathlib source files are not
1103	  % mirrored in the project's reference cache). The mathematics is standard.
1104	  For each \(i \in \{0, 1\}\), the ring map
1105	  \[ \mathrm{mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway}_i \colon
1106	     \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k \longrightarrow
1107	     \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \]
1108	  --- the inverse direction of the chart-ring iso of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso},
1109	  built via \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial.eval}_2\mathrm{Hom}\) as the ring map sending the
1110	  generator \(u \in \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k\) to the homogeneous-localisation
1111	  element \(X_{1-i}/X_i \in \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) and the constants
1112	  \(r \in \bar k\) along the composite $\bar k \to \mathcal A_0 \to \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal
1113	  A\,X_i\( --- is surjective onto \)\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$.
1114	  \medskip
1115	  \textit{Proof.} The image of \(\mathrm{mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway}_i\)
1116	  is the \(\mathcal A_0\)-subalgebra of \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) generated by the
1117	  single localised element \(X_{1-i}/X_i\), since \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k\)
1118	  is generated as a \(\bar k\)-algebra by its single generator \(u\) and the base-ring map
1119	  \(\bar k \to \mathcal A_0\) is bijective (via the canonical identification of degree-zero
1120	  scalars in the \(\bar k\)-algebra \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) with \(\bar k\)). Mathlib's lemma
1121	  \(\mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin\_mk\_prod\_pow\_eq\_top}\) says that for any
1122	  multi-index \(v \colon \iota' \to A\) of homogeneous elements of degree \(dv\) generating
1123	  \(\mathcal A\) up to degree \(d\), the \(\mathcal A_0\)-subalgebra of
1124	  \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(\prod v_i^{dv_i})\) generated by the family of ratios
1125	  \(\{v_i^{dv_i}/\prod v_j^{dv_j}\}\) is the whole ring \(\top\). Specialising to
1126	  \(d = 1\), \(\iota' = \mathrm{Fin}\,2\), \(v = (X_0, X_1)\), and exponent vector
1127	  \(dv = (1, 1)\), the relevant generator \(\prod v_i^{dv_i} = X_0 \cdot X_1\) collapses
1128	  along the localisation at \(X_i\) (the \(X_i\)-component is already a unit, so only the
1129	  \(X_{1-i}/X_i\) ratio remains as a non-trivial generator), and the conclusion is that
1130	  \(\mathrm{Algebra.adjoin}\,\mathcal A_0\,\{X_{1-i}/X_i\} = \top\) inside
1131	  \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\). This is exactly the image of
1132	  \(\mathrm{mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway}_i\), so the map is surjective. \qed
1133	\end{lemma}
1134	\begin{lemma}
1135	\leanok
1136	[Chart-ring iso preserves \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\)]
1137	  \label{lem:chart_ring_iso_preserves_algebraMap}
1138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap}
1139	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the \(\bar k\)-algebra-preservation half of the chart-ring iso
1140	  % (\cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso}). The cited Mathlib fact
1141	  % \texttt{HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap\_eq} is a structural unfolding of how the
1142	  % \(\bar k\)-algebra structure on \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) is wired through
1143	  % \texttt{Algebra.compHom} from \(\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom}\), not a
1144	  % statement copied from a textbook; no external % SOURCE QUOTE is retained because the
1145	  % project does not mirror Mathlib source files under \texttt{references/}.
1146	  For each \(i \in \{0, 1\} = \mathrm{Fin}\,2\), the forward direction of the chart-ring iso
1147	  of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} preserves the canonical \(\bar k\)-algebra structure:
1148	  composing
1149	  \[ \mathrm{forward}_i \colon
1150	     \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i
1151	     \longrightarrow
1152	     \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k \]
1153	  with the \(\bar k\)-algebra map \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i)\)
1154	  on the source side yields the canonical
1155	  \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k)\) on the target side:
1156	  \[ \mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i)
1157	     \,\fatsemi\,
1158	     \mathrm{forward}_i
1159	     \ =\
1160	     \mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k). \]
1161	  Equivalently, the chart-ring iso sends each constant element \(r \in \bar k\) --- viewed as
1162	  a degree-\(0\) element of \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) via
1163	  \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i)\) --- to the corresponding
1164	  constant \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial.C}\,r \in \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k\).
1165	  \medskip
1166	  \noindent\textbf{Proof sketch.} The \(\bar k\)-algebra map
1167	  \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i)\) unfolds (via Mathlib's
1168	  \(\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap\_eq}\)) as the
1169	  \(\mathrm{Algebra.compHom}\) of \(\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom}\) along the
1170	  canonical \(\bar k \to \mathcal A_0\) inclusion of constants into the degree-\(0\) piece of
1171	  the standard \(\mathbb N\)-graded ring \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\). The forward direction
1172	  \(\mathrm{forward}_i\) of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} sends a degree-\(0\) element of
1173	  \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) to its image under the chart evaluation \(X_i \mapsto 1\),
1174	  \(X_{1-i} \mapsto u\); on a constant \(r \in \bar k\) this evaluation is exactly
1175	  \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial.C}\,r\). The identification of the two ring maps on every constant
1176	  \(r\) is therefore the round-trip identity
1177	  \(\mathrm{forward}_i \circ \mathrm{inverse}_i = \mathrm{id}_{\bar k[u]}\) (the axiom-clean
1178	  half of \cref{lem:proj_chart_ring_iso_aux_left}) applied to the constant
1179	  \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial.C}\,r\), together with the surjectivity of
1180	  \(\mathrm{inverse}_i = \mathrm{mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway}_i\)
1181	  (\cref{lem:mvPoly_to_homogeneousLocalization_away_surjective}) that lifts the
1182	  pointwise check to a ring-map equality. This is the load-bearing \(\bar k\)-algebra
1183	  preservation step consumed by Step (B) of \cref{lem:gmscaling_chart_PLB_eq}, where the
1184	  chart-\(i\) ring map is post-composed with \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\) of the relative
1185	  tensor product to identify it with
1186	  $\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \otimes_{\bar k}
1187	  \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k)$.
1188	\end{lemma}

1554	\begin{lemma}
1555	\leanok
1556	[\(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is reduced]
1557	  \label{lem:projlinebar_isReduced}
1558	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_isReduced}
1559	  % Archon-original Lean realisation of an elementary textbook fact (projective space over
1560	  % a field is reduced) via the explicit chart cover of \cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1561	  % and the canonical \(\mathrm{val}\)-injection of a homogeneous localization into the
1562	  % corresponding plain localization. No external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1563	  The scheme underlying \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is reduced. By the two-chart affine cover
1564	  (\cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}) it suffices to show that each chart ring is
1565	  reduced. The chart-\(i\) ring \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) embeds into the ordinary
1566	  localization \(\bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]\) of the polynomial ring \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) at
1567	  the powers of \(X_i\) via the canonical injection $\mathrm{val} \colon
1568	  \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \hookrightarrow \bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$
1569	  (\(\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.val\_injective}\)); the codomain is a localization of a
1570	  polynomial ring at a non-zero-divisor (the powers of \(X_i\)), hence a domain, hence
1571	  reduced. A subring of a reduced ring is reduced
1572	  (\(\mathtt{Function.Injective.isDomain}\) on the \(\mathrm{val}\)-embedding), so each chart
1573	  ring is a domain --- in particular reduced --- and the global reducedness follows.
1574	\end{lemma}

1578	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}

183	Each declaration below is project-local (general-purpose graded ring `𝒜 : ℕ → σ`); none
184	mention `kbar` or `projectiveLineBarGrading`. They are placed here as supplements
185	because they have no upstream Mathlib home (yet). -/
186	
187	/-- Project-local supplement: the preimage of `Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f` under
188	`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` is `⊤`. Combines `Proj.opensRange_awayι` with
189	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange`. -/
190	lemma Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self
191	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
192	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
193	    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤ := by
194	  rw [← Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm, Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange]
195	
196	/-- Project-local supplement: `Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` factors as
197	`Spec.map (basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ (isAffineOpen_basicOpen ...).fromSpec`.
198	
199	This is the "iter-195 analogist recipe step (i)" `Proj.awayι_isoSpec_compat` from
200	`analogies/lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot.md`. It is the bridge that lets us transport
201	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (which is about `Γ(X, U)`, not the
202	project's `Away 𝒜 f`) into a closed-form for `(Proj.awayι).app`. -/
203	lemma Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec
204	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
205	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
206	    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm =
207	      Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
208	      (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).fromSpec := by
209	  rw [Proj.awayι, IsAffineOpen.fromSpec, ← Category.assoc]
210	  congr 1
211	  -- Goal: `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv`.
212	  -- Repackage RHS as the `inv` of a single `Iso.trans` so we can apply `Iso.ext`
213	  -- on the `.hom` side, where the `basicOpenIsoSpec_hom`/`basicOpenToSpec`/
214	  -- `isoSpec_hom = toSpecΓ` chain composes definitionally.
215	  rw [show (Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
216	       (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec.inv) =
217	       ((Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec ≪≫
218	         Scheme.Spec.mapIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).op).inv from rfl]
219	  congr 1
220	  apply Iso.ext
221	  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec, Iso.trans_hom,
222	      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom]
223	  rfl

225	/-! ### Iter-193 Lane E refactor — `IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq` route
226	
227	The substantive content of `iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval` (the chart-1 `Proj.appIso`
228	evaluation `isLocElem ↦ 1`) repeatedly STUCK across iter-188/189/190/191 on the
229	`Proj.appIso` evaluation step. Iter-193 reroutes via `IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq`:
230	since `iotaGm_r_1 = IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.awayι X_1) onePt.left h_range` by
231	definition, *any* `Spec.map(F)` satisfying the same lift identity must equal
232	`iotaGm_r_1`. So if we can build an explicit ring map
233	`kbarChart1Ring : Away 𝒜 X_1 →+* kbar`
234	sending `isLocElem ↦ 1` and prove `Spec.map kbarChart1Ring ≫ Proj.awayι X_1 =
235	onePt.left`, then `iotaGm_r_1 = Spec.map kbarChart1Ring` (by `lift_uniq`), and
236	the whole chart-1 evaluation reduces to a ring-map identity on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`. -/
237	
238	/-- **Iter-193 Lane E (helper).** The chart-`1` evaluation ring map at the `k̄`-point
239	`[1:1]`: `Away 𝒜 X_1 →+* k̄` sending the chart-`1` affine coordinate
240	`isLocElem = [X_0/X_1]` to `1 ∈ k̄`.

242	Defined as the composition
243	`Away 𝒜 X_1 →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* k̄`
244	where the first map is the chart-`1` ring iso forward direction
245	(`homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar 1`, sending `isLocElem ↦ X () = u`)
246	and the second is `MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ ↦ 1)` (evaluation of the chart-`1`
247	affine coordinate `u` at `λ = 1`). -/

90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance

151	/-! ### Project-local Mathlib supplement — MvPolynomial as standard smooth algebra
152	
153	This subsection ships an axiom-clean construction of the canonical submersive
154	presentation of `MvPolynomial ι R` as an `R`-algebra (no relations, generators
155	indexed by `ι`). Mathlib does not ship a direct instance
156	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)`
157	(per iter-182 prover scout); we build it here from the underlying
158	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` machinery. This is a pure
159	`Mathlib`-supplement: it depends only on `Mathlib`, and is independent
160	of the rest of the project.
161	-/
162	
163	/-- The canonical `Generators` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` indexed by `α`,
164	with `val := MvPolynomial.X` and section `σ' := id`. -/
165	noncomputable def mvPolyGenerators (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
166	    Algebra.Generators R (MvPolynomial α R) α :=
167	  Algebra.Generators.ofSurjective MvPolynomial.X <|
168	    fun s => ⟨s, MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left_apply s⟩
169	
170	/-- The canonical `Presentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` with no relations
171	(`σ = PEmpty`) and generators indexed by `α`. The kernel of the natural
172	`aeval X` map is `⊥` since `aeval X` is the identity. -/
173	noncomputable def mvPolyPresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
174	    Algebra.Presentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
175	  __ := mvPolyGenerators R α
176	  relation := PEmpty.elim
177	  span_range_relation_eq_ker := by
178	    simp only [Set.range_eq_empty, Ideal.span_empty]
179	    rw [Algebra.Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val]
180	    change ⊥ = RingHom.ker (MvPolynomial.aeval (R := R) (MvPolynomial.X : α → MvPolynomial α R))
181	    rw [MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]
182	    ext x
183	    simp [RingHom.mem_ker]
184	
185	/-- The canonical `PreSubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
186	The differential is on the zero module `PEmpty → P.Ring`. -/
187	noncomputable def mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
188	    (α : Type w) :
189	    Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
190	  __ := mvPolyPresentation R α
191	  map := PEmpty.elim
192	  map_inj := fun a _ _ => PEmpty.elim a
193	
194	/-- The canonical `SubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
195	The Jacobian is the determinant of the zero-sized matrix, which is `1` (a unit). -/
196	noncomputable def mvPolySubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
197	    (α : Type w) :
198	    Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
199	  __ := mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation R α
200	  jacobian_isUnit := by
201	    rw [Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation.jacobian_eq_jacobiMatrix_det]
202	    simp [Matrix.det_isEmpty]
203	
204	/-- **Mathlib supplement**: `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R` is `R`-standard smooth of
205	relative dimension `n`. Built from `mvPolySubmersivePresentation` with
206	dimension `Nat.card (Fin n) - Nat.card PEmpty = n`. -/
207	instance mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
208	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) :
209	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
210	  (mvPolySubmersivePresentation R (Fin n)).isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension <| by
211	    simp [Algebra.Presentation.dimension]

230	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
231	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
232	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈

246	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
247	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family

250	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
251	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
252	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
253	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
254	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
255	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
256	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
257	    (m := ![1, 1])
258	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
259	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
260	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
261	    (by
262	      classical
263	      intro p hp
264	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
265	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
266	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
267	        have := hp
268	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
269	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
270	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
271	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
272	        exact this
273	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
274	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
275	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
276	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
277	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
278	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
279	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
280	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
281	        by_contra h
282	        push Not at h
283	        apply hd_ne
284	        ext k
285	        fin_cases k
286	        · simpa using h.1
287	        · simpa using h.2
288	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
289	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
290	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
291	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
292	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
293	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
294	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
295	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
296	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
297	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
298	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
299	
300	/-! ### `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instance via the 2-chart cover -/
301	
302	/-- **Per-chart smoothness gap for `ℙ¹_{k̄}` over `Spec k̄`** (iter-196 substrate).
303	After the 2-chart cover reduction via `IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of_openCover`, this
304	lemma is the per-chart gap: the composition of the chart open immersion with the
305	structure morphism is smooth of relative dimension `1`.
306	
307	The proof reduces (via `HasRingHomProperty.iff_of_isAffine` for the affine source
308	and target) to a `RingHom.Locally (IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1)` statement
309	on the composite ring map `k̄ → Γ(chart_i, ⊤)`. Closing this gap requires an algebra
310	isomorphism `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄ ≃ₐ[k̄] Γ(chart_i, ⊤)` — this iso is built in
311	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso` as `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`
312	(downstream of this file), so the closure is gated on either (i) duplicating the iso
313	here (~80 LOC) or (ii) relocating the smoothness instance to a downstream file.
314	The substrate `mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` (this file)
315	handles the "polynomial-ring side" axiom-clean. -/
316	private lemma projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
317	    (i : Fin 2) :
318	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
319	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) := by
320	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.iff_of_isAffine (P := @SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1)]
321	  -- Reduce `RingHom.Locally` to direct `RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1`
322	  -- using the `RespectsIso` shipping of `RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`.
323	  apply RingHom.locally_of RingHom.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_respectsIso
324	  -- Closing this remaining goal needs the chart-ring iso
325	  --   `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar ≃ₐ[kbar] Γ(chart_i, ⊤)`
326	  -- combined with `mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` (this file)
327	  -- and `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv` (Mathlib). The
328	  -- chart-ring iso is `ChartIso.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` (downstream of this
329	  -- file). Closure-when-ChartIso-is-in-scope: at most ~10 LOC.
330	  sorry
331	
332	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold.
333	iter-196 substrate landed: the structural reduction via the 2-chart cover is done
334	axiom-clean; the remaining per-chart gap is isolated in
335	`projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux`. -/
336	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
337	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
338	  apply IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of_openCover (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover
339	  exact projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux kbar
340	
341	end AlgebraicGeometry
342	
343	end

\begin{lemma}
[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is geometrically irreducible]
  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
  % II.4 + I.1; \(\Proj\) of an integral domain over a field is
  % integral and geometrically integral when the field is algebraically
  % closed and the polynomial ring is well-defined). No external SOURCE
  % QUOTE needed.
  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
  .\mathrm{hom} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is
  geometrically irreducible: for every field extension \(K/\bar k\),
  the base change \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \times_{\Spec \bar k} \Spec K
  = \mathbb P^1_K\) is irreducible. Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically
  closed and \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is the polynomial ring (integral
  domain), \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is
  integral, and the base change to any field extension \(K\) preserves
  integrality (the homogeneous coordinate ring \(K[X_0, X_1]\) is
  itself an integral domain, hence \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is integral and
  irreducible).
\end{lemma}
input.new_string:
\begin{lemma}
[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is geometrically irreducible]
  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
  % II.4 + I.1; \(\Proj\) of an integral domain over a field is
  % integral and geometrically integral when the field is algebraically
  % closed and the polynomial ring is well-defined). No external SOURCE
  % QUOTE needed.
  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
  .\mathrm{hom} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is
  geometrically irreducible: for every field extension \(K/\bar k\),
  the base change \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \times_{\Spec \bar k} \Spec K
  = \mathbb P^1_K\) is irreducible. Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically
  closed and \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is the polynomial ring (integral
  domain), \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is
  integral, and the base change to any field extension \(K\) preserves
  integrality (the homogeneous coordinate ring \(K[X_0, X_1]\) is
  itself an integral domain, hence \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is integral and
  irreducible).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
  % NOTE (iter-197 blueprint-writer): the iter-196 lean-vs-blueprint-checker
  % flagged this proof as under-specified for Lean closure. The expanded sketch
  % below decomposes the argument into five named sub-helpers (A--E), pinning
  % the load-bearing Proj-base-change iso (Helper A) to a future Lean target;
  % until Helper A lands, the public `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` instance
  % remains a Tier-3 named scaffold sorry.
  We decompose the argument into five sub-helpers. The load-bearing piece is
  Helper~A (the Proj base-change isomorphism), which is currently a Mathlib gap
  at the pinned commit \(\mathtt{b80f227}\) and must be supplied as a project-side
  substrate (estimated 100--200 LOC). The remaining helpers are then either
  Mathlib-direct or short combinatorial bridges. Total estimated closure cost
  across the chain: 200--350 LOC over multiple iters.
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~A (load-bearing): base-change of \(\Proj\).}
  \(\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.baseChangeIso}\) (future target;
  Mathlib upstreaming candidate or project supplement). For a graded ring
  \(\mathcal A \colon \mathbb N \to \sigma\) over a base ring \(R\) and an
  \(R\)-algebra \(K\), there is a natural isomorphism of schemes over \(\Spec R\):
  \[ \Proj\,(K \otimes_R \mathcal A)
     \ \cong\ \Proj\,\mathcal A \,\times_{\Spec R}\, \Spec K. \]
  This is the standard Stacks Project base-change formula for relative
  \(\Proj\) (Stacks tag 0BLW, ``base change of relative Proj''). At the pinned
  Mathlib commit \(\mathtt{b80f227}\) no version of this isomorphism is shipped
  for the abstract graded-ring \(\Proj\) construction; the project-side build
  needs to construct the iso functorially, prove naturality in \(K\), and
  expose the \(\Spec K\)-projection compatibility for downstream consumers.
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~B: chart-iso reparameterisation over an extension
  field.} \(\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.baseChange}\)
  (future target). The chart-ring iso of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso}
  identifies \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \cong \bar k[u]\) over the base
  field \(\bar k\). Helper~B re-runs the same construction over an arbitrary
  field extension \(K / \bar k\) to obtain
  \(K \otimes_{\bar k} \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \cong K[u]\) (the affine
  chart of \(\mathbb P^1_K\)). This is a routine base-change of the iso of
  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and carries no new substrate gap once
  Helper~A is in scope.
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~C: the base-changed graded ring is a graded
  integral domain.} For \(K\) any field extension of \(\bar k\), the tensor
  product \(K \otimes_{\bar k} \bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is a graded \(K\)-algebra
  canonically identified with the polynomial ring \(K[X_0, X_1]\), which is
  itself an integral domain. Consequently the underlying scheme of
  \(\Proj\,(K \otimes_{\bar k} \bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is integral on every basic
  open \(D_+(X_i)\) (each chart ring is a localisation of the integral domain
  \(K[X_0, X_1]\), hence a domain), and a pointwise check at generic points
  gives integrality of the whole scheme. (Currently un-pinned; iter-197+ prover
  names the Lean target as it materialises the helper.)
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~D: \(\Proj\) of an integral graded domain over a
  field is integral.} Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Proj.instIsIntegral}\)-style instance (when the irrelevant-ideal
  cofinality assumption holds, as it does for the standard polynomial grading)
  packages the global integrality of \(\Proj\,(K[X_0, X_1])\). Integrality
  implies irreducibility of the underlying scheme. (Currently un-pinned;
  iter-197+ prover names the Lean target as it materialises the helper.)
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~E: assemble via Helper~A.} For every field
  extension \(K / \bar k\), Helper~A gives
  \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k) \times_{\Spec \bar k} \Spec K
  \cong \Proj\,(K \otimes_{\bar k} \bar k[X_0, X_1]) = \mathbb P^1_K\); Helper~C
  + Helper~D give that \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is integral, hence irreducible.
  Combined with the algebraic-closure hypothesis on \(\bar k\) (which makes the
  generic-point check uniform across all extensions), this gives
  \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\,(\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k).\mathrm{hom}\).
  (Currently un-pinned; iter-197+ prover names the Lean target as it
  materialises the helper.)
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Mathlib references and gap budget.} Stacks tag 0BLW (Proj
  base-change) is \emph{not} in Mathlib \(\mathtt{b80f227}\); project-side
  substrate is required and is the dominant cost (Helper~A,
  \(\sim 100\)--\(200\) LOC). Mathlib does ship
  \(\mathtt{Proj.instIsIntegral}\) (Helper~D) and the
  \(\mathrm{Algebra.TensorProduct}\) machinery (Helper~C) but \emph{not} the
  graded-tensor-product-of-polynomials integral-domain instance directly, so
  Helper~C requires a brief project-side bridge. Total estimated closure cost:
  200--350 LOC across the five helpers; Helper~A dominates. This sorry is
  acceptable as a Tier-3 named scaffold until the Proj base-change
  infrastructure lands.
\end{proof}

% whose proof shape transports verbatim. The three blocks below capture the
% port and its consumer dispatch.
% NOTE (iter-197 blueprint-writer): the iter-196 prover identified the
% intermediate \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\top\)
% identity as the missing named helper whose absence caused the dependent-motive
% blocker on \cref{lem:awayi_app_basicOpen}. It is now pinned below as
% \cref{lem:basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top}, immediately preceding the section-level
% port.
\begin{lemma}

[Inversion of \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}\) at \(\top\)]
  \label{lem:basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top}

  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top}
  % Project-bespoke section-level intermediate isolated from the proof of
  % \cref{lem:awayi_app_basicOpen}. The iter-196 prover's task report explicitly
  % named this as the missing helper whose absence caused the dependent-motive
  % blocker on `Scheme.Hom.app` (`.archon/task_results/AlgebraicJacobian/
  % AbelianVarietyRigidity.md:66-71`). Closed form via the same Mathlib
  % `Scheme.invApp` + `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom` + `Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top`
  % chain; no external textbook source.
  Let \(\mathcal A \colon \mathbb N \to \sigma\) be an \(\mathbb N\)-graded ring,
  let \(f \in \mathcal A_m\) be a homogeneous element of strictly positive
  degree \(m > 0\), and let \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}\,\mathcal A\,f\,
  (\mathtt{f\_deg})\,(\mathtt{hm})\) be the canonical iso of schemes
  \(\Proj.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f \,\cong\,
  \Spec\,(\mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\,\mathcal A\,f)\). Then the
  inverse evaluated at the whole space \(\top\) admits the closed-form
  factorisation
  \begin{align*}
    (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,
       (\mathtt{hm})).\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\top
    \ =\ \;
      &(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\mathrm{topIso.hom} \\
      &\quad\fatsemi\,
      (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoAway}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,
        (\mathtt{hm})).\mathrm{inv} \\
      &\quad\fatsemi\,
      (\mathrm{Scheme}.\Gamma\mathrm{SpecIso}\,\_).\mathrm{inv}.
  \end{align*}
  Equivalently, the inverse of the scheme iso \(\mathrm{basicOpenIsoSpec}\),
  evaluated on the whole space \(\top\), factors as the chain
  \(\mathrm{topIso}\)-followed-by-inversions of the two component isos
  (chart-ring iso and \(\Gamma\mathrm{SpecIso}\)).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{}
  Set \(\beta := \mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})
  \,(\mathtt{hm})\). By Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Scheme.invApp}\) (the
  inverse-of-iso lemma for scheme presheaf maps), the section
  \(\beta.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\top\) is the categorical inverse of the
  ring map \(\beta.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,\top\) on the appropriate
  presheaf. Substitute the explicit value
  \(\beta.\mathrm{hom} = \mathrm{Proj.basicOpenToSpec}\,\mathcal A\,f\,
  (\mathtt{f\_deg})\,(\mathtt{hm})\) via Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec\_hom}\). Then evaluate
  \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenToSpec}.\mathrm{app}\,\top\) via Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Proj.basicOpenToSpec\_app\_top}\), which exposes the explicit
  factor chain
  \[ (\mathrm{Scheme}.\Gamma\mathrm{SpecIso}\,\_).\mathrm{hom}
     \,\fatsemi\,
     \mathrm{awayToSection}\,\mathcal A\,f
     \,\fatsemi\,
     (\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f).\mathrm{topIso.inv}, \]
  where \(\mathrm{awayToSection}\,\mathcal A\,f\) is the canonical (forward)
  ring map that \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoAway}.\mathrm{hom}\) is implemented
  as. Inverting this composition (composition of isos reverses) yields the RHS
  of the lemma statement. The simplification is mechanical
  (\(\mathtt{Iso.inv\_hom\_id}\) cancels each factor's round trip in turn);
  LOC estimate: \(\sim 5\)--\(15\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Preimage of \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\) under \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι}\) is \(\top\)]
  \label{lem:awayi_preimage_basicOpen_self}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self}
  % Project-local Mathlib supplement (axiom-clean, iter-196 landed). Combines
  % Mathlib's \(\mathrm{Proj.opensRange\_awayι}\) with
  % \(\mathrm{Scheme.Hom.preimage\_opensRange}\). No external textbook source.
  Let \(\mathcal A \colon \mathbb N \to \sigma\) be an \(\mathbb N\)-graded ring,
  \(f \in \mathcal A_m\) homogeneous of degree \(m > 0\). The preimage of the
  basic open \(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f\) under the canonical
  Spec-morphism \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,
  (\mathtt{hm})\) is the whole space:
  \[ \mathrm{Proj.awayι}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,(\mathtt{hm})
       \,\Vert^{-1}\,\mathrm{Proj.basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f
     \ =\ \top. \]
  This is a direct corollary of the range of \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι}\) being
  exactly \(D_+(f)\), via the canonical
  \(\mathrm{Scheme.Hom.preimage\_opensRange}\) rewrite. \(\sim 2\) LOC in Lean.
\end{lemma}

  % textbook source: the substantive content is the categorical
  % `Iso.ext`-via-`basicOpenIsoSpec_hom` cancellation; the proof shape is
  % project-bespoke.
  Let \(\mathcal A \colon \mathbb N \to \sigma\) be an \(\mathbb N\)-graded ring,
  \(f \in \mathcal A_m\) homogeneous of degree \(m > 0\). Then the canonical
  Spec-morphism \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι}\) factors through Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{IsAffineOpen.fromSpec}\) via the chart-ring iso
  \(\mathrm{basicOpenIsoAway}\):
  \[ \mathrm{Proj.awayι}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,(\mathtt{hm})
     \ =\
     \Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{Proj.basicOpenIsoAway}\,\mathcal A\,f\,
       (\mathtt{f\_deg})\,(\mathtt{hm})).\mathrm{inv}
     \,\fatsemi\,
     (\mathrm{Proj.isAffineOpen\_basicOpen}\,\mathcal A\,f\,(\mathtt{f\_deg})\,
       (\mathtt{hm})).\mathrm{fromSpec}. \]
  The proof unfolds \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι}\) and \(\mathtt{IsAffineOpen.fromSpec}\)

951	\begin{lemma}
952	[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is smooth of relative dimension \(1\)]
953	  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim}
954	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim}
955	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
956	  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
957	  % II.8.20 for projective space smoothness over a field; the relative
958	  % dimension is the projective dimension n=1 for \(\mathbb P^1\)). No
959	  % external SOURCE QUOTE needed.
960	  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
961	  .\mathrm{hom} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is smooth
962	  of relative dimension \(1\). The proof uses the standard 2-chart
963	  affine cover by \(D_+(X_0)\) and \(D_+(X_1)\) (each chart is
964	  \(\Spec(\bar k[t])\) where \(t\) is the affine coordinate
965	  \(X_i/X_{1-i}\)); each chart is standard smooth via the polynomial
966	  ring presentation, hence smooth over \(\Spec \bar k\); the relative
967	  dimension equals \(1\) via the single generator \(t\) of the
968	  polynomial ring. The smoothness on each chart lifts to smoothness on
969	  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) via the open-cover gluing
970	  (\texttt{Smooth\char`_iff\char`_atOpens}).
971	\end{lemma}
972	\begin{lemma}
973	[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is geometrically irreducible]
974	  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
975	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
976	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
977	  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
978	  % II.4 + I.1; \(\Proj\) of an integral domain over a field is
979	  % integral and geometrically integral when the field is algebraically
980	  % closed and the polynomial ring is well-defined). No external SOURCE
981	  % QUOTE needed.
982	  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
983	  .\mathrm{hom} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is
984	  geometrically irreducible: for every field extension \(K/\bar k\),
985	  the base change \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \times_{\Spec \bar k} \Spec K
986	  = \mathbb P^1_K\) is irreducible. Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically
987	  closed and \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is the polynomial ring (integral
988	  domain), \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is
989	  integral, and the base change to any field extension \(K\) preserves
990	  integrality (the homogeneous coordinate ring \(K[X_0, X_1]\) is
991	  itself an integral domain, hence \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is integral and
992	  irreducible).

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is proper over \(\Spec \bar k\)]
  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_isProper}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_isProper}
  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
  % II.4 + the standard properness of Proj of a finitely generated graded
  % algebra over a Noetherian base; here the base is a field, hence trivially
  % Noetherian). No external SOURCE QUOTE needed.
  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k).\mathrm{hom}
  \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is proper.
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Proof sketch.} Unfold \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
  .\mathrm{hom}\) as the composition
  \(\Proj.\mathrm{toSpecZero}\,(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading}\,\bar k)
  \fatsemi \Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,
  \uparrow{\mathcal A_0})\). The first factor is proper by Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero}\): the polynomial algebra
  \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is finite type over its degree-\(0\) piece
  \(\mathcal A_0\), and the \(\mathtt{IsScalarTower}\) +
  \(\mathtt{Algebra.FiniteType}\) chain (via
  \(\mathtt{Algebra.FiniteType.of\_restrictScalars\_finiteType}\)) supplies the
  finite-type witness. The second factor \(\Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}
  \,\bar k\,\uparrow{\mathcal A_0})\) is an isomorphism: the algebra map
  \(\bar k \to \uparrow{\mathcal A_0}\) is bijective because \(\mathcal A_0\)
  is the constants subalgebra of \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) (injectivity via
  \(\mathtt{MvPolynomial.C\_injective}\); surjectivity via
  \(\mathtt{MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent\_of\_mem}\) +
  \(\mathtt{homogeneousComponent\_zero}\)). Composition of proper with iso is
  proper. LOC: \(\sim 40\) (already landed axiom-clean).
\end{lemma}

[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is smooth of relative dimension \(1\)]
  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim}
  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, lem:mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}
  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
  % II.8.20 for projective space smoothness over a field; the relative
  % dimension is the projective dimension n=1 for \(\mathbb P^1\)). No
  % external SOURCE QUOTE needed.
  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
  .\mathrm{hom} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is smooth
  of relative dimension \(1\). The proof uses the standard 2-chart
  affine cover by \(D_+(X_0)\) and \(D_+(X_1)\) (each chart is
  \(\Spec(\bar k[t])\) where \(t\) is the affine coordinate
  \(X_i/X_{1-i}\)); each chart is standard smooth via the polynomial
  ring presentation, hence smooth over \(\Spec \bar k\); the relative
  dimension equals \(1\) via the single generator \(t\) of the
  polynomial ring. The smoothness on each chart lifts to smoothness on
  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) via the open-cover Zariski-locality criterion
  for \(\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\), namely Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of\_openCover}\) (specialised to
  \(\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,1\) via the
  \(\mathtt{HasRingHomProperty} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsZariskiLocalAtSource}\)
  chain).
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Per-chart substrate dependency.} The per-chart closure
  decomposes as: (i) reduce smoothness to a
  \(\mathtt{RingHom.Locally\,IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension\,1}\)
  statement via Mathlib's \(\mathtt{HasRingHomProperty.iff\_of\_isAffine}\)
  (both source and target are affine); (ii) reduce \(\mathtt{Locally}\) to the
  direct property via
  \(\mathtt{RingHom.locally\_of\,isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension\_respectsIso}\);
  (iii) close on the composite \(\bar k \to \Gamma(\mathrm{chart}_i, \top)\)
  ring map by combining Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of\_algEquiv}\) with
  the project's \cref{lem:mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}
  (the \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial\,(Fin\,n)\,R}\) instance) transported along the
  chart-ring iso \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso}
  (\(\mathtt{homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}\)) reparameterised as an
  \(\bar k\)-algebra equivalence \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,(\mathrm{Fin}\,1)\,
  \bar k \cong \Gamma(\mathrm{chart}_i, \top)\).
  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Status (iter-196).} The cover-reduction structural step is
  landed axiom-clean. The per-chart aux (Lean:
  \(\mathtt{projectiveLineBar\_smooth\_chart\_aux}\), private) carries the
  residual sorry, because the chart-ring iso
  \(\mathtt{homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}\) lives in
  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean}\), which is
  \emph{downstream} of \(\mathtt{BareScheme.lean}\) — so the per-chart closure
  cannot consume it without an import cycle. \textbf{Recommended refactor:}
  relocate \(\mathtt{projectiveLineBar\_smoothOfRelDim}\) (and its private
  per-chart aux) to \(\mathtt{ChartIso.lean}\) or a new
  \(\mathtt{Genus0BaseObjects/Smooth.lean}\) where the chart-ring iso is in
  scope; then close the per-chart gap via
  \(\mathtt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of\_algEquiv}\) in
  \(\sim 10\) LOC. This relocation is being dispatched separately to the
  refactor subagent (slug \(\mathtt{barescheme\text{-}smoothness\text{-}relocation}\))
  this iter; once landed, the residual sorry closes.
\end{lemma}
\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Project-side substrate: \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,(\mathrm{Fin}\,n)\,R\) is
\(R\)-standard smooth of relative dimension \(n\)]
  \label{lem:mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}
  % Project-local Mathlib supplement (axiom-clean, iter-196 landed). Builds the
  % canonical SubmersivePresentation of MvPolynomial as an R-algebra (no
  % relations; generators indexed by Fin n). No external textbook source: the
  % construction is pure category-of-presentations infrastructure on the
  % Mathlib `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` machinery.
  For any commutative ring \(R\) and natural number \(n\), the polynomial ring
  \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,(\mathrm{Fin}\,n)\,R\) is \(R\)-standard smooth of
  relative dimension \(n\). At the pinned Mathlib commit \(\mathtt{b80f227}\)
  Mathlib does \emph{not} ship a direct instance
  \(\mathtt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,n\,R\,
  (\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,(\mathrm{Fin}\,n)\,R)\) (per iter-182 prover scout);
  the project supplies it via a 5-declaration
  \(\mathtt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\) chain (Lean names with
  \(\mathtt{Algebra}\)-namespace target):
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \(\mathtt{mvPolyGenerators}\) — canonical generators
      (\(\mathtt{val} := \mathrm{MvPolynomial.X}\); section \(\sigma' := \mathrm{id}\)).
    \item \(\mathtt{mvPolyPresentation}\) — presentation with empty relation set
      (\(\sigma = \mathtt{PEmpty}\); kernel of \(\mathrm{aeval}\,\mathrm{X}\) is \(\bot\)).
    \item \(\mathtt{mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation}\) — pre-submersive with empty
      \(\mathtt{map} \colon \mathtt{PEmpty} \to \alpha\).
    \item \(\mathtt{mvPolySubmersivePresentation}\) — submersive presentation;
      Jacobian determinant of the zero-sized matrix is \(1\) (a unit).
    \item \(\mathtt{mvPolynomialFin\_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\) —
      the final instance, obtained from
      \(\mathtt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\)
      with dimension count \(\mathrm{Nat.card}\,(\mathrm{Fin}\,n)
      - \mathrm{Nat.card}\,\mathtt{PEmpty} = n\).
  \end{enumerate}
  Total LOC across the chain: \(\sim 50\) (all axiom-clean, iter-196 landed).
  This substrate is the load-bearing input to the per-chart smoothness step of
  \cref{lem:projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim}.
\end{lemma}
